prompt: I think AAPL is going to rally before its next earnings in about 6 weeks. The stock is at $195 right now. IVR on AAPL is about 18. What options strategy should I use, what strikes, what expiration, and when should I take profit or cut losses?

assertions.text: Recommends a bullish strategy appropriate for low IVR (long call or bull call spread, NOT selling premium)
Explicitly mentions that low IVR (18) makes buying options favorable
Provides specific strike prices or delta guidance (e.g. ATM, 0.50 delta, or a specific dollar strike)
Recommends 30-60 DTE expiration to balance theta decay and time for the move
Gives both a profit target AND a stop loss / exit rule
Mentions position sizing or risk management (% of account or contract count)
